description: Restrict world-state broadcast recipients to active world members derived from membership state, use each member's own endpoints for delivery, and add regression coverage for both exclusion and inclusion paths.

userStories.title: Inspect current world-state broadcast targeting
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the existing broadcastWorldState recipient-selection flow documented so that the privacy fix can replace the unsafe path without broad refactoring.
acceptanceCriteria: The implementation notes identify the current recipient-selection logic in packages/agent-world-sdk/src/world-server.ts.
The notes confirm whether broadcastWorldState currently iterates over peerDb.values() for recipient targeting.
The notes identify agentLastSeen and agentEndpoints as the membership structures available for recipient derivation.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Inspection completed in packages/agent-world-sdk/src/world-server.ts. broadcastWorldState() currently builds knownPeers from [...peerDb.values()].filter((p) => p.endpoints?.length) and then iterates those peer records for delivery. The world server already tracks active membership separately via agentLastSeen (Map<string, number>) and agentEndpoints (Map<string, { alias: string; endpoints: Endpoint[] }>), and getMembers() already treats membership as the intersection of those two structures.

userStories.title: Add membership-based recipient selection
userStories.description: As a developer, I need broadcast recipient IDs derived from active membership state so that only joined agents are eligible for world-state delivery.
acceptanceCriteria: broadcastWorldState no longer uses peerDb.values() to determine recipient agent IDs.
Recipient selection is derived from agentLastSeen entries for the world.
Only agent IDs with at least one endpoint entry in agentEndpoints are returned as eligible recipients.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added getBroadcastRecipientIds() in packages/agent-world-sdk/src/world-server.ts so broadcastWorldState() now derives recipient agent IDs from agentLastSeen and only keeps IDs that still have at least one endpoint entry in agentEndpoints. The broadcast path no longer iterates peerDb.values() to choose recipients.

userStories.title: Isolate eligible member endpoints for broadcast
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a per-member endpoint lookup in the broadcast path so that each selected member is paired only with its own registered endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: The broadcast logic resolves endpoints from agentEndpoints for the currently selected member ID.
Members without endpoints are skipped without attempting delivery.
The code no longer reuses endpoint lists from unrelated peer records when preparing world-state sends.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Updated packages/agent-world-sdk/src/world-server.ts so broadcastWorldState() resolves endpoints from agentEndpoints for each eligible recipient ID. Members without registered endpoints are skipped, and the loop no longer reuses peerDb endpoint lists that may belong to unrelated discovered peers.

userStories.title: Route sends through the existing delivery path
userStories.description: As a joined agent, I need world-state snapshots delivered through the existing send mechanism so that the privacy fix preserves successful delivery behavior for valid members.
acceptanceCriteria: The inner broadcast loop still calls the existing world-state send path for each eligible endpoint.
A member with multiple registered endpoints triggers one send attempt per owned endpoint.
No new delivery mechanism bypasses the current broadcast send implementation.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Kept broadcast delivery on the existing signed HTTP `/peer/message` path in packages/agent-world-sdk/src/world-server.ts, but changed the per-member loop to attempt every owned endpoint instead of returning after the first successful send. Added test/world-state-broadcast.test.mjs to verify an active member with two registered endpoints receives a world.state send attempt on both.

userStories.title: Add a non-member broadcast exclusion test fixture
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a focused test setup that models a known peer without active world membership so that the privacy leak can be verified in isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: A test fixture or setup in test/*.test.mjs represents a peer known to discovery but absent from the world's active membership state.
The fixture includes peer data that would have been reachable under the pre-fix peerDb-based selection path.
The test setup builds from dist output following the repository's test conventions.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added a reusable non-member fixture helper in test/world-state-broadcast.test.mjs that seeds discovery through a signed /peer/announce without calling world.join. The fixture asserts the announced peer lands in the peer list with a reachable endpoint, matching the shape that the old peerDb-based broadcast selection would have used.

userStories.title: Verify non-members do not receive world-state broadcasts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need regression coverage for denied delivery so that peers without active membership cannot silently receive world snapshots again.
acceptanceCriteria: A test asserts that a known peer without an agentLastSeen membership entry does not receive a world-state broadcast.
The test verifies that no send attempt is made to endpoints owned only by the non-member peer.
The test fails against the pre-fix peerDb-based recipient selection and passes with the membership-based implementation.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Extended test/world-state-broadcast.test.mjs with a regression that announces a reachable peer without sending world.join, waits across several broadcast intervals, and asserts no /peer/message send attempt is made to that non-member endpoint. The test fails under the old peerDb recipient-selection path and passes with membership-based targeting.

userStories.title: Verify active members still receive broadcasts on their own endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need regression coverage for allowed delivery so that active joined members continue receiving world-state updates after the privacy fix.
acceptanceCriteria: A test asserts that an agent present in agentLastSeen with at least one endpoint in agentEndpoints receives a world-state broadcast.
The test verifies delivery uses the joined member's own endpoint data rather than another peer's endpoints.
The test covers a member with multiple endpoints or explicitly verifies iteration over all registered endpoints.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Strengthened test/world-state-broadcast.test.mjs so the positive regression seeds a discovered non-member alongside a joined member, intercepts outbound /peer/message sends, and asserts that world.state delivery hits exactly the active member's registered ports. The test still covers multi-endpoint delivery and now also proves endpoint ownership by excluding the non-member endpoint from the observed hit set.

userStories.title: Align existing broadcast coverage with membership-only behavior
userStories.description: As a developer, I need existing broadcast tests reviewed and updated only where necessary so that the new membership requirement is enforced without weakening coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing world-state broadcast tests continue to run with assertions intact unless an assertion conflicts with the new membership-only rule.
Any updated expectation explicitly reflects that only active joined members are valid broadcast recipients.
No existing assertion is removed solely to make the new implementation pass.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run build and full test validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need project validation completed after the code and regression updates so that the change is ready for review and autonomous execution.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run build succeeds after the recipient-selection and test changes.
node --test test/*.test.mjs succeeds after building.
The final diff includes the membership-based broadcast targeting change and regression coverage for both denied and allowed delivery paths.
Typecheck passes
